title: Warehouse Manager

rationales.taskAutomation: Shift scheduling and throughput reporting are increasingly AI-generated, but running a physical warehouse floor — safety, labor management, equipment issues — requires on-site human judgment.
rationales.toolMaturity: Warehouse management system (WMS) analytics and AI forecasting tools are credible and improving, but do not replace floor-level operational management.
rationales.adoption: Common in large logistics and retail distribution operations for planning support; the management function itself is not being automated away.
rationales.agenticExposure: Scheduling and reporting are agent-ownable slices; core physical-floor leadership and safety accountability are not.

tasksAtRisk: Routine shift scheduling and labor allocation
tasksGrowing: Frontline team leadership and safety culture
Crisis response to equipment failure or labor shortages
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 11-3071 (Transportation, Storage, and Distribution Managers), p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI now handles the scheduling math and throughput reporting, but running a physical floor safely still takes a person who can read the room and react to a forklift breakdown. That keeps this role solidly Low risk.
